---
title: "X_DataTables"
output: html_document
---

########################################## 
#                                        #
#          GMA Project - Tables          #
#                                        #
########################################## 


```{r}
setwd("~/Desktop/Doutorado UFV/FIT 799 - Pesquisa/Scripts/GMA Scripts")
load("MeansTable.RData")
source("./0_Formulas.R")

#packages
library(clipr)
library(tidyverse)
library(kableExtra)
```

### Generate LaTeX table of variance components
```{r}
# Reshape data to wide format
variance_fmtd <- variance |>
  mutate(Dataset = factor(Dataset,
                         levels = levels(variance$Dataset),
                         labels = c("Original", "Predicted"))) |> 
  mutate(Trait = factor(Trait,
                         levels = levels(variance$Trait),
                         labels = c(traits$name)))

# Create LaTeX-friendly column names
colnames(variance_fmtd) <- c(
  "Trait",
  "Dataset",
  "$\\sigma^2_{env}$", 
  "$\\sigma^2_{gF_{2}}$", 
  "$\\sigma^2_{a}$", 
  "$\\sigma^2_{d}$", 
  "$h^2_{b}$",
  "$h^2_{n}$"
)


latex_table <- kable(
  variance_fmtd,
  format = "latex",
  booktabs = TRUE,
  caption = "Variance components and heritability estimates across original and predicted datasets. Values represent variance components ($\\sigma^{2}$) and heritability estimates ($h^{2}$).",
  label = "tab:variance_components",
  row.names = FALSE,
  escape = FALSE
) |> 
collapse_rows(columns = 1, valign = "middle") |> 
  kable_styling(position = "center")

# Print the LaTeX code of variance components
clipr::write_clip(latex_table)
```

### Generate LaTeX table of GMA additive+dominance and epistatic model
```{r}
latex_table <- list()

traitsEPI <- traits |> 
  filter(trait %in% c(
    "num_sideshoot",
    "total_leaf_sideshoot",
    "total_cluster_sideshoot",
    "leaf_angle"
  ))

# traitsEPI <- traits |> filter(trait %in% c(
#   "total_leaf_sideshoot",
#   "internode_length",
#   "lai"    
#   ))

for (i in 1:nrow(traitsEPI)){
  
  gma_fmtd <- gma_list[[traitsEPI$trait[i]]]  
  
  gma_fmtd <- gma_fmtd  |> 
    select(!Trait) |> 
    mutate(Dataset = factor(Dataset,
                            levels = levels(gma_fmtd$Dataset),
                            labels = c("Original", "Predicted"))) |> 
    mutate(model = factor(model,
                            levels = levels(gma_fmtd$model),
                            labels = c(
                              paste0("A+D ($", levels(gma_fmtd$model)[1], ")\\textsuperscript{1}$"),
                                       "Epistatic",
                              paste0("A+D ($", levels(gma_fmtd$model)[3], ")\\textsuperscript{1}$")
                              ))) |> 
    mutate(parameters = factor(parameters,
                            levels = levels(gma_fmtd$parameters),
                            labels = c("$\\hat{a}$",
                                       "$\\hat{d}$",
                                       "$\\hat{m}$",
                                       "$\\hat{aa}$",
                                       "$\\hat{ad}$",
                                       "$\\hat{dd}$"
                                       )))
  
  colnames(gma_fmtd) <- c(
    "Dataset",
    "Model ($r^{2}$)",
    "$\\hat{a_{i}}$", 
    "Estimates", 
    "$\\sigma^2_{\\hat{a_{i}}}$", 
    "$\\sigma_{\\hat{a_{i}}}$", 
    "$t$-test", 
    "$df$", 
    "$p$-value", 
    "Sum of squares",
    "$R^{2}$(\\%)"
  )
  
  # Generate LaTeX table with booktabs formatting
  latex_table[[i]] <- kable(
    gma_fmtd,
    align = "lllcccccccc",
    valign = "[t]",
    format = "latex",
    booktabs = TRUE,
    table.envir = "sidewaystable",
    caption = paste0("Parameter ($\\hat{a_{i}}$) estimates for ", tolower(traitsEPI$name[i])," under additive-dominance (A+D) and epistatic models using original and predicted datasets. Values include estimates variance ($\\sigma^2_{\\hat{a_{i}}}$), standard deviation ($\\sigma_{\\hat{a_{i}}}$), significance levels, degrees of freedom ($df$), sum of squares, and relative parameter contributions ($R^{2}$) for each model."),
    label = paste0(i+1, "gma:", traitsEPI$trait[i]),
    row.names = FALSE,
    escape = FALSE
  ) |> 
    collapse_rows(columns = c(1,2), valign = "middle") |> 
    footnote(
      number = c("Predictive ability of generation means with the A+D model.")
      ) |> 
    kable_styling(position = "center")
  
}

# Print the LaTeX code of GMA tables for additive+dominance and epistatic models
clipr::write_clip(latex_table)
```

### Generate LaTeX table of GMA additive+dominance model
```{r}
latex_table <- list()

traitsEPI <- traits |> filter(trait %in% c(
 "num_sideshoot",
 "total_leaf_sideshoot",
 "total_cluster_sideshoot",
 "leaf_angle"
  ))

traitsAD <- setdiff(traits, traitsEPI)

for (i in 1:nrow(traitsAD)){
  
  gma_fmtd <- gma_list[[traitsAD$trait[i]]]  
  
  gma_fmtd <- gma_fmtd  |> 
    select(!Trait) |> 
    filter(model != "epi") |> 
    droplevels()
  gma_fmtd <- gma_fmtd  |> 
    mutate(Dataset = factor(Dataset,
                            levels = levels(gma_fmtd$Dataset),
                            labels = c("Original", "Predicted"))) |> 
    mutate(model = factor(model,
                          levels = levels(gma_fmtd$model),
                          labels = c(
                            paste0("A+D ($", levels(gma_fmtd$model)[1], ")\\textsuperscript{1}$"),
                            paste0("A+D ($", levels(gma_fmtd$model)[2], ")\\textsuperscript{1}$")
                          ))) |> 
    mutate(parameters = factor(parameters,
                               levels = levels(gma_fmtd$parameters),
                               labels = c("$\\hat{a}$",
                                          "$\\hat{d}$",
                                          "$\\hat{m}$"
                               )))
    
  colnames(gma_fmtd) <- c(
    "Dataset",
    "Model ($r^{2}$)",
    "$\\hat{a_{i}}$", 
    "Estimates", 
    "$\\sigma^2_{\\hat{a_{i}}}$", 
    "$\\sigma_{\\hat{a_{i}}}$", 
    "$t$-test", 
    "$df$", 
    "$p$-value", 
    "Sum of squares",
    "$R^{2}$(\\%)"
  )
  
  # Generate LaTeX table with booktabs formatting
  latex_table[[i]] <- kable(
    gma_fmtd,
    align = "lllcccccccc",
    valign = "[t]",
    format = "latex",
    booktabs = TRUE,
    # table.envir = "sidewaystable",
    caption = paste0("Parameter ($\\hat{a_{i}}$) estimates for ", tolower(traitsAD$name[i])," under additive-dominance (A+D) models using original and predicted datasets. Values include estimates variance ($\\sigma^2_{\\hat{a_{i}}}$), standard deviation ($\\sigma_{\\hat{a_{i}}}$), significance levels, degrees of freedom ($df$), sum of squares, and relative parameter contributions ($R^{2}$) for each model."),
    label = paste0(i+1, "gma:", traitsAD$trait[i]),
    row.names = FALSE,
    escape = FALSE
  ) |> 
    collapse_rows(columns = c(1,2), valign = "middle") |> 
    footnote(
      number = c("Predictive ability of generation means with the A+D model.")
    ) |> 
    kable_styling(position = "center")
  
}


# Print the LaTeX code of GMA tables for additive+dominance and epistatic models
clipr::write_clip(latex_table)
```
##Figures labels
#Variograms (individually)
```{r eval=FALSE, include=FALSE}
vario <- paste0("\\begin{figure}[htbp]
  \\centering
  \\includegraphics[width=1.0\\textwidth, angle=-90]{figs/vario_", traits$trait, ".eps}
  \\caption{Variogram of ", tolower(traits$name), " using the autoregressive correlation residual structure and irrigation spatial covariate.}\\label{fig:vario_", traits$trait, "}
\\end{figure}
")
clipr::write_clip(vario)
```

#Variograms 6x2 block
```{r}
lista <- list()

n <- n+6

traits$print <- paste0("    \\begin{subfigure}{0.45\\textwidth}
       \\centering
       \\includegraphics[width=\\linewidth, angle=-90]{figs/vario_", traits$trait,".eps}
       \\caption{",traits$name,"}
    \\end{subfigure}")

lista[["beg"]] <- "\\begin{figure}[htbp]
  \\centering\n"

for(j in (n):(n+5)){
  lista[[paste0(j,1)]] <- traits$print[j]
  if (j%%2 == 1){
    lista[[paste0(j,2)]] <- "    \\hfill"
  } else {
    if (j == (n+5)){
      
    } else {
      lista[[paste0(j,2)]] <- "\n"
    }
  }
}

lista[["end"]] <- "  \\caption{Variograms of trait measurements using an autoregressive residual correlation structure and irrigation spatial covariate, showing how variance changes with distance. The $z$-axis represents the variance across different distances (lag) based on row (line) and plant position (plant n°).}
  \\label{fig:vario_onetosix}
\\end{figure}"


clipr::write_clip(paste0(lista))
```
